name: Donny Hathaway
abstract: Donny Edward Hathaway (October 1, 1945 – January 13, 1979) was an American soul singer, keyboardist, songwriter, backing vocalist, and arranger who Rolling Stone described as a "soul legend". His most popular songs include "The Ghetto", "This Christmas", "Someday We'll All Be Free", and "Little Ghetto Boy".

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Donny Hathaway/ Herbie Mann Quintet and The Family of Mann
Newport Jazz Festival: Donny Hathaway and Black Heat with David Newman

about_quotes.speaker: Joel Dorn
about_quotes.evidence: Listen I had a hit with Roberta Flack and Donny Hathaway that Arif Martin and I co- produced. It was called “Where is the Love.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Joel Dorn interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: I actually wrote a song with him which I found out later he recorded.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: He’s got about five or six gold records, my son. Also, at this time, he ended up making me great. I said, “I always wanted to be great.” So, I got four great grandchildren. They are…you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Heath — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
